video_title: How to (and not to) check oil level on Land Rover Discovery 4 LR4
transcript: so we've got a new not not brand new but we've got a new car to us our new project car the land R Discovery 4 and we thought we check the oil on it we bought it without checking the oil and it didn't take us long to work out that on this 3 l tdv6 there is no oil dipstick I thought that might be it but it's not and then we look and you've got to actually check your oil level on the dash um there's a little it's a little bit complicated how to do it but not too bad so we thought we'd do a little video on how to do it but we've also found out there's a way not to do it which the other video we found on YouTube doesn't show you so we've actually started the engine and just run the car I'll just do it now oh gosh there we go so we started the engine right we stopped the car right cuz um to get to this menu what you got to do is you've got to not put your foot on the brake and press the start key which turns the ignition on okay so the key is not to press the start key then you've got to press this okay button here okay got the Bonnet Open Press okay to clear come on ah all right you press okay enough and sooner or later you'll come up with this little menu here now you can get this menu with the car running but with the car not running you get this extra little menu here here that says service menu so if you go into this you can check your VIN number your chassis number but we want the oil display okay so sorry I'm moving these up and down to Circle between that so there go if I select the okay for the oil display now the key here is I've got this and I oh no look my oil's on minimum it's terrible but the key here they really shouldn't show you it's not available now the reason it's not available is cuz you've just run the engine and there's some sort of tricky timer in the ECU that doesn't show you your oil level until you've not run the car for 5 or 10 minutes so we're going to go and have a cup of tea come back and see what the oil level says um in a minute so that's the so if you're going to do this don't start your engine or or do it after you've not run your engine we'll come back in a minute and see what it says so I've waited 10 minutes I've had my cup of tea um um so don't press the brake again press the start all the ignition comes up okay we got my Bonnet open so I've got to clear that error so hopefully you can see both bits of the screen press okay press break when starting I don't want to do that press okay Bonnet open okay press okay again there we go and then we're going to scroll down here to the service menu you might just be able to see that at the top of the screen hopefully you can press okay right go down to the oil display right it's still saying not available okay so maybe I got to wait a bit more um I'll see what we need to do okay okay it's now 11:08 um I waited another minute and we seem to have it now so if you bu one more time we'll go down to the service menu oil level display and the oil level you can now see that it doesn't say not available here and it's actually shown we're full which may be because I've overfilled it but anyway I'll sort that problem out but that's how not to and how to do it properly um check your oil level on [Music] the Discovery 4 3 l TD V6 hope that helps
